font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">has_</code><span>&nbsp;</span>to name a function that returns a true/false value, it is often referred to as<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Predicate Naming</strong>. This style makes the code read like a question or a statement of fact, which is standard practice in languages like<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Python</strong>,<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Ruby</strong>, and<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">C</strong>.<span><span style="white-space: nowrap; </span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;"># Verb-Noun naming (Performing an action)</span><span>
</span><span style="color: rgb(197, 138, 249);">def</span><span> </span><span style="color: rgb(250, 144, 62);">fetch_user_data</span><span>(</span><span style="color: rgb(250, 144, 62);">user_id</span><span>):

</span><span style="color: rgb(154, 160, 166); font-style: italic;"># Using both together</span><span>

    </span><span style="color: rgb(250, 144, 62);">data</span><span> = fetch_user_data(current_user.id)
    print(</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">f</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">Data received: </span><span>{data}</span><span style="color: rgb(129, 201, 149);">"</span><span>)
</span></code></pre></div></div><div style="display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px 4px 16px 16px; margin-top: 3px; overflow: hidden; padding: 12px 16px;"><div style="font-size: 12px;">Use code with caution.</div><button style="margin: 0px; background: none; color: inherit; border: none; font: inherit; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer; height: 44px; width: 44px; display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; border-radius: 50%;"></button></div></div></div><div></div><div role="heading" style="margin-block: 24px 12px; font-family: &quot;Google Sans&quot;, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 20px; font-weight: var(--JT4IPc); line-height: var(--qYGXw); letter-spacing: 0px; color: var(--jINu6c);">3. Key Naming Rules<span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px; cursor: pointer;"><span style="color: var(--IXoxUe); display: inline-block; transform: rotate(135deg);"></span></button></span></span></div><ul style="margin: 0px; padding: 0px; margin-block: 12px 16px; font-family: var(--nkmQOe); font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 24px; letter-spacing: 0px; padding-inline-start: 16px;"><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Avoid "Get" for Booleans:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Instead of<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">get_is_prime</code>, just use<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">is_prime</code>. Using "get" implies you are retrieving an object, while "is" implies a true/false condition.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Stay Positive:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>Use affirmative names like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">is_enabled</code><span>&nbsp;</span>instead of<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">is_not_disabled</code>. This makes logic like<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">if not is_enabled:</code><span>&nbsp;</span>much easier to read than<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">if not is_not_disabled:</code>.</span></li><li style="margin: 0px 0px 12px; padding: 0px; list-style: disc; padding-inline-start: 4px;"><span style="overflow-wrap: break-word;"><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Match your Data:</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>If the function returns a list or object, use a<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Noun</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Verb-Noun</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">user_list</code><span>&nbsp;</span>or<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">get_users</code>). If it returns a boolean, use the<span>&nbsp;</span><strong style="font-weight: 700;">Predicate</strong><span>&nbsp;</span>(e.g.,<span>&nbsp;</span><code dir="ltr" style="unicode-bidi: isolate; font-size: 14px; line-height: 22px; background-color: var(--XKMDxc); border: 1px solid var(--XKMDxc); border-radius: 4px; padding: 2px 4px;">has_users</code>).</span><span><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span>&nbsp;</span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; text-decoration-style: initial; text-decoration-color: initial;"><div style="display: contents;">You can summarize this style guide as:<span>&nbsp;</span></div><strong style="font-weight: 700;">"Use Verb-Noun for actions and Predicate (is/has) for boolean states."</strong><span style="visibility: hidden;"><span style="white-space: nowrap; position: relative;"><span></span><button tabindex="0" style="margin: 0px 6px 0px 0px; background-color: var(--aYn2S); border: none; border-radius: 10px; height: 20px; padding: 0px; width: 20px; outline: 0px;
